name: Dhansak (Chicken with Lentils and Vegetables)
recipeInstructions:
MASALA:
4 tablespoons Ghee or clarified butter
1 large onion, sliced
1 1/2 inches Ginger, peeled and sliced
2 Green Chillis (Cayennes?)
3 Garlic cloves, crushed
1/2 teaspoon Cinnamon, ground
1/2 teaspoon Cardamom, ground
1/2 teaspoon Cloves, ground
1 1/2 teaspoons Turmeric, ground
1 Teaspoon Coriander, ground
1/2 teaspoon Hot Chili Powder
3 Tablespoons Coriander Leaves, chopped
Wash all the dals thoroughly and soak for 30 minutes in some cold water. Drain and transfer to a large saucepan. Add the 3 3/4 cups Water and Salt and bring to a boil, skimming off any scum. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 40 minutes.
Meanwhile, fry the Ginger and Garlic in a frying pan with Ghee until fragrant. Add Chicken and fry until deeply and evenly browned. Transfer this mixture to the dal mixture.
Add the Mint, Aubergine, Pumpkin, Spinach, Onion, and Tomatoes. Increase heat and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes, or until Chicken is cooked through and tender. Remove Chicken pieces to a plate. Puree the Dal and Vegetables in a blender and set aside.
To make the Masala, clean the pan and fry Onion in Ghee until golden. Stir in Ginger, Chillis, and Garlic and fry for 3 minutes. Stir in all the remaining ingredients except Coriander Leaves and fry constantly for 8 minutes. A little water may be added if the mixture becomes too dry. Pour the Pureed Dal and Veg mixture into the Masala and mix well. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes. Stir in Chicken and simmer for further 10 minutes, basting the Chicken with the sauce.
Transfer to a warm serving dish and garnish with Chopped Coriander Leaves.
